models.label: LCDM / Unimodular
models.description: w = -1 exactly; Lambda is a cosmological constant (GR) or an integration constant (unimodular gravity). Identical predictions at classical level.
models.K_note: 1 parameter (Lambda value) to ~12 sig-figs = ~40 bits. Unimodular: same, reinterpreted as boundary condition.

models.label: Quintessence (typical)
models.description: Slowly-rolling scalar field. w0 != -1. CPL point w0=-0.95, wa=-0.3 is typical for tracker models.
models.K_note: 2 CPL parameters (~80 bits) + scalar field potential specification (~200 bits). Total ~280 bits above LCDM baseline.

models.label: Running vacuum (fitted)
models.description: Lambda(H) = Lambda0 + 3*nu*(H^2 - H0^2). Effective CPL: w0=-0.97, wa=-0.15 for nu~1e-3.
models.K_note: 1 free parameter nu (dimensionless, ~32 bits precision) plus Lambda0 (~same as LCDM). Total incremental ~40 bits.

models.label: Phantom (w < -1)
models.description: w < -1 (phantom crossing). Requires ghost scalar or modified gravity. wa = 0 assumed.
models.K_note: 1 free parameter w0 (~32 bits) plus ghost/modified gravity specification. Incremental ~40 bits above LCDM.

bic_analysis.bic_verdict_from_paper: LCDM preferred by BIC even using DESI's own chi2 improvement

k_information_summary.conclusion: DESI 2024 reports delta(-2lnL) = 14.3 for w0waCDM vs LCDM. BIC penalty for 2 extra parameters = 15.20. Net delta_BIC = 0.90. Since delta_chi2 > BIC penalty, BIC marginally prefers CPL using DESI's reported chi2 — but the margin is small (~0.9 BIC units). Under K-informationalism: Occam penalty (K-bits added by quintessence = 280 bits vs 40 bits for LCDM) is a separate, stronger consideration. A ~3.9 sigma statistical preference for w != -1 does not yet justify adding 240 bits of K-content (field specification). K-informationalism supports LCDM/unimodular until evidence is both statistically and informationally compelling.
